mnemonics.title: Arithmetic Sequence Formula
mnemonics.content: Aardvark Needs Dinner - A + (N-1)D. The nth term equals first term plus (n-1) times common difference

mnemonics.title: Geometric Sequence Formula
mnemonics.content: Giraffes Run - Geometric: a_n = a_1 × r^(n-1). First term times radius (ratio) to the (n-1) power

mnemonics.title: Arithmetic Series Sum
mnemonics.content: Nice Flies - n/2 × (first + last) or n/2 × (2a + (n-1)d). Average of first and last, multiplied by number of terms

mnemonics.title: Geometric Series Sum (r≠1)
mnemonics.content: Ganesh Eats Raw Bananas - Geometric sum: a(1-r^n)/(1-r). Remember: 'First one, minus last one, over one minus ratio'

mnemonics.title: Infinite Geometric Series
mnemonics.content: Infinite Is Easy - a/(1-r) only when |r|<1. Think: 'Auntie Divide' - divide first term by (1 minus ratio)

mnemonics.title: Summation Properties
mnemonics.content: Sums Are Linear - Σ(c×a_i + d×b_i) = c×Σa_i + d×Σb_i. Constants factor out, sums split apart (SUM = S + U + M)

acronyms.usage: Use when dealing with sequences that increase/decrease by a constant difference
Use when dealing with sequences that multiply by a constant ratio each step
The Σ symbol means 'sum of' - write the expression below and above the sigma
When reading Σ(i=1 to n), remember: i is the variable, 1 is the starting value, n is the ending value
Σc = nc (sum of constant c repeated n times)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine stairs climbing evenly - each step is the same height above the previous. Think of a ladder with equal rungs.
Imagine a photograph being enlarged by the same factor each time - 2x, 4x, 8x, 16x... like compound interest growing exponentially.
Think of Σ as stacking rectangles - each term is the height of a bar, summing them builds a total area under a curve.
Picture the Greek letter Σ as a sideways 'M' for 'More terms to add' - the expression below shows where to start, above shows where to stop.
Imagine a ball bouncing with decreasing height - if each bounce is a fraction of the previous (|r|<1), the ball eventually stops. The total distance sums to a finite number.

analogies.analogy: Like earning a fixed salary increment each year - your salary increases by Rs 5000 every year, forming an arithmetic progression.
Like a bacteria culture doubling every hour - 2, 4, 8, 16, 32... each term multiplies by the same factor (ratio), like compound interest.
Like adding up your monthly expenses over a year - the sigma (Σ) is like saying 'add up all these monthly amounts from January to December'.
Like finding the total of all numbers on a calendar page - average the first and last day, multiply by number of days.
Like a ping-pong ball bouncing with each bounce reaching 1/2 the height of the previous - the heights form a geometric sequence, and the total distance traveled approaches a limit.
Think of filling a bucket - a convergent series is like a bucket with a hole (water settles to a level), a divergent series is like a bucket with no bottom (water keeps rising forever).
